description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two jobs. The pages job runs in the default deploy stage using the asciidoctor/docker-asciidoctor image, runs only on the master branch, and executes the exact shell commands: apk add --no-cache nodejs nodejs-npm openssh-client rsync ruby ruby-rdoc ruby-irb ruby-dev build-base gcc libffi-dev git; npm install -g clean-css-cli; gem install bundler; ./build-docs.sh; cd blog; gem install bundler; bundle update --bundler; bundle; bundle exec jekyll build; mv _site ../output/blog; bundle exec jekyll build --drafts; mv _site ../output/blog_drafts; cd ..; mv output public; cp logo.svg public; cp slides/* public; cp icons/* public. It then publishes the public directory as a GitLab Pages artifact. The verify_signatures job runs in the default test stage using the ubuntu:18.04 image and runs the exact shell commands: apt update; apt install --assume-yes python-pexpect gnupg2 git; gpg2 --list-keys; (gpg2 --export --no-default-keyring --keyring ./.gnupg/pubring.kbx --verbose || true) | gpg2 --import --verbose; gpg2 --list-keys --fingerprint --fingerprint --verbose; ./verify-signatures.py HEAD ./.mailmap, with no artifacts or branch restrictions.
